title: HTTP/2 Frames, Streams, HPACK, Flow Control, and Go Boundaries · Language Lens: H2 Preface Settings Frames
summary: A team assumes HTTP/2 is compressed HTTP/1.1 text, shares one parser state across streams, ignores SETTINGS and flow control, indexes secrets into HPACK, and maps every stream failure to connection failure. Planned artifact: Add and verify the h2 preface settings frames increment in HTTP/2 connection, stream, frame, and compression model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace connection preface, SETTINGS exchange and acknowledgement, frame header, payload length, type, flags, stream identifier, and connection errors.
Run race, leak, allocation, benchmark, profiling, and changed-load gates while separating correctness from capacity claims.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict H2 Preface Settings Frames · Theory H2 Preface Settings Frames
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 1477 in a protocol upgrade gateway transferring buffered tunnel bytes. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine HTTP/2 frame, stream, flow-control, and header-list bounds; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace connection preface, SETTINGS exchange and acknowledgement, frame header, payload length, type, flags, stream identifier, and connection errors.
